feedback.text: I would like to say that video games were always looked down upon as a medium and it is true that just like any media there is good and bad, but the preservation of that medium is necessary if we are to give our history to the future generation. Just as much as music, movies, sculptures, paintings and other art forms should be preserved, that is why I humbly come forward to ask you to add a way for paying customers to still use the product that they buy even after the companies are done supporting them, forcing a end of life plan for video games would assure a way for them to remain playable after they are retired from the servers. Things like giving the documentation and the files necessary to make servers on the community side for always online games, or creating an update for singleplayer games disabling the anti piracy measures once it's taken off the plublisher support to let people still use the products they bought, obviously this should not be retroactive as sadly many companies simply went bankrupt making this prospect impossible, but for currently running games that is absolutly possible and while yes updating current games with those measures would take ressources from the publishers and developpers, future games would have to be built around the regulation thus making that process way cheaper. I hope that this small comment will help in any way it can into making up your decision, i wish you a good morning/ afternoon/ night
